event.description:
-------How to Join our online/Virtual event ------
- Before the meetup visit https://www.sfbayareatech.xyz/ and enter your details to join our slack community. Once you submit your email address and name you will receive an invite to join so check your email.
- Once you have joined our slack community please join the #Reactive channel
- RSVP to this event here on Meetup
- Location part of this event page is a Zoom link to join the event. You will only be able to see the link if you have RSVP to this event.
- Any questions please contact the organizers (Alexy or Salar) via our slack community or meetup.
------PLEASE FOLLOW ABOVE Instructions before event---------

Reactive programming with RxJava and Spring Reactor is a powerful technique for building scalable asynchronous systems. In this session you will learn about Reactive-gRPC, a library that combines the simplicity of gRPC microservices with the powerful asynchronous programming model of RxJava.

gRPC has native support for streaming services, with built-in flow control to handle backpressure automatically. Reactive-gRPC seamlessly bridges the gRPC backpressure model with RxJava, helping you build backpressure aware streaming applications from the API all the way down to the wire, and provides a unified programming model across server, desktop, and mobile.

In this session, you will learn:
How to use the RxJava and gRPC streaming APIs,
How the RxJava, gRPC, and HTTP/2 flow control models work together to create backpressure aware microservices, and
How Reactive-gRPC simplifies building highly asynchronous systems and unifies the programming model for server, desktop, and mobile applications.

Bio: Ryan Michela is a principal member of the technical staff at Salesforce, where he’s working to integrate the Salesforce ecosystem with microservices. His passions are distributed systems and helping other developers become better. When he’s not digging into the heart of software, Ryan enjoys hiking and exploring the world.
event.title: Reactive Microservices End-to-End with gRPC
